extra.reasoning:
The property requires that rec_drug_a and rec_drug_b are never jointly true.

rec_drug_a is set true only by r5 (priority 50), whose condition is the full 8-flag conjunction C = (anticoagulated AND asthma AND low_ejection_fraction AND ckd_dialysis AND g6pd_deficiency AND diabetes AND elderly AND hepatic_impairment). No rule sets rec_drug_a false, and the default is false. So rec_drug_a = true iff C holds.

Now consider rec_drug_b precisely when C holds (the only scenario in which a can be true). Two rules write rec_drug_b: r2 (priority 90) IF C THEN rec_drug_b := false, and r3 (priority 50) IF anticoagulated THEN rec_drug_b := true. When C holds, anticoagulated is true, so both fire. Conflict resolution is by highest priority, and r2 (90) beats r3 (50), forcing rec_drug_b := false.

Therefore, in every input where rec_drug_a = true (i.e., C true), rec_drug_b is forced false by the higher-priority r2. The remaining inputs (immunosuppressed, seizure_history) only affect rec_drug_c via r1/r4 and cannot influence a or b. Hence rec_drug_a AND rec_drug_b is unsatisfiable. The mutual-exclusion property holds across the entire 2^10 input space.
